weather_report:
Osborne Park wakes under a quiet lid of cloud, the light soft and silvery, edges of buildings smudged as if drawn in charcoal. Through late morning into early afternoon the overcast holds steady, the sun a shy conspirator behind gauze. Expect a dim, even brightness rather than sparkle; the UV is muted by the cloud, gentler than a clear Perth day, though around midday it can still nip unguarded skin.

It’s a day for light layers and long sleeves, the sort you forget you’re wearing until the breeze reminds you why. Sunglasses are optional; a touch of sunscreen is prudent if you’ll be out a while. The mood suits unhurried errands, a library stop, a coffee by a window, or a short walk where the flat light turns colors into calm pastels. If you’re working from home, crack a window and let the cool drift in; if you’re commuting, the ride will feel mercifully glare-free. Nothing dramatic is expected, just a gentle, gray companion to your Wednesday.
